snippet.title: Chinese Fans Are Attacking This US-Born Figure Skater Who Chose To Compete For China Then Fell
snippet.description:
Zhu Yi, a 19-year-old US-born figure skater who gave up her US citizenship to compete for China at the Beijing Olympics, is facing intense backlash on Chinese social after she fell during her debut on Sunday Feb. 6.

As the only women’s singles entry for the team skate, Zhu competed in both women’s segments.

Her short program ended in disaster after she crashed into the barriers in her opening jump sequence and missed a jump later.

Team China, who was vying for a bronze medal, fell to 5th. Its standing was solidified a day later during Zhu’s free skate program, when she fell two more times.

#ZhuYiFellOver quickly became a trending hashtag on Chinese social media platform Weibo before it was deactivated, but not before racking up over 200 million views, according to CNN.

A second hashtag #ZhuYiMessedUp remains on Weibo with over 80 millions views.

Many users questioned Zhu’s selection in the team event while others criticized her for not being able to speak fluent Mandarin.

“Please let her learn Chinese first, before she talks about patriotism,” one Weibo user wrote.

“I’m upset and a little embarrassed,” Zhu said to reporters after her skate. “I guess I felt a lot of pressure because I know everybody in China was pretty surprised with the selection for ladies’ singles and I just really wanted to show them what I was able to do but unfortunately I didn’t.”

Fans around the world have since taken to social media to defend Zhu.

“Zhu Yi doesn’t deserve the bullying she’s receiving for something as senseless as a fall. She is & will always be Chinese, regardless of where she was born,” one user wrote.

Born to Chinese parents in the US as Beverly Zhu, Zhu changed her name to Zhu Yi and relinquished her US citizenship in 2018 to represent China as part of the country’s “Morning Road” initiative for recruiting athletes for the 2022 Winter Olympics.

She is one of several foreign-born athletes to decide to compete for China including fellow Chinese American freestyle skier Eileen Gu, who has become the unofficial face of the Games in China and a gold medal favorite in women’s ski slopestyle.

snippet.title: This South Korean Soccer Player Who Scored Two Goals Is So Handsome People Can’t Handle It
snippet.description:
The World Cup has its newest heartthrob, and it’s South Korean striker Cho Gue-sung, who has been making waves on social media for being extremely handsome.

Fan cams and photos of the charismatic No. 9 have gone viral after he scored South Korea’s two goals in the match against Ghana on Monday Nov. 28.

A fancam video that shows Cho sitting on the sidelines with his arms crossed then rubbing his cheek has garnered more than 8.3 million views on Twitter.

The hashtag #ChoGuesung on TikTok has 382 million views, with fans gushing over Cho.

The 24-year-old’s Instagram account has catapulted from 20,000 followers at the beginning of the World Cup to more than 2.3 million fans as of Sunday Dec. 4.

Cho reportedly had to turn off his phone from the multitude of notifications and messages he was getting.

“He had to get some rest,” Seo Jung-hwan, a South Korean journalist, told the Athletic. “His phone was going all night. It was keeping him awake. He was trying to concentrate on football and, all the time, the messages were coming.”

Cho’s newfound celebrity status has many speculating about his future opportunities in the form of brand partnerships, modeling work and TV commercials.

Cho’s meteoric rise to international fame has been compared to the mania from the 2002 World Cup, when South Korean midfielder Kim Nam-il became an overnight sensation.

Fans will next see Cho play on Monday Dec. 5 when South Korea plays Brazil in the Knockout stage.

snippet.title: Meta Horizon Worlds! Ancient China: A tour of Metaverse Chang’an city!
snippet.description:
To celebrate the AAPI heritage month, HuaPlus collaborated with the creators and designers of the Meta Horizon Worlds "Chang'An, China (7th Century)", bringing up this immersive VR experience to our communities. In this VR world, you can hang out with friends, watch horse wagons passing by in the West Market, and get a taste of the capital city of the Tang dynasty as you fly over it. Come and join Cosmos and Shuyi in the video tour of this Metaverse Chang’an city!

Horizon Worlds
  "Chang'An, China (7th Century)"
https://www.oculus.com/vr/5085735434781500/

长安城VR项目介绍:

中国传统文化主题 Horizon Worlds
  "Chang'An, China (7th Century)"
https://www.oculus.com/vr/5085735434781500/
元宇宙里还原7世纪的唐朝古都长安


已实现的一些空间功能：

让游客能变身功夫高手的可穿戴道具
丰富的唐朝小吃，和好朋友一起杯酒话人生
为保存玄奘法师由天竺经丝绸之路带回长安的经卷佛像而建的大雁塔，
在这个可爱的动画世界里被赋予了全新的功能，
游客可以从大雁塔的顶端出发，乘坐仙鹤，游览整个长安


长安VR项目制作团队:

Song 宋辰翔

Product Designer @ Oculus

Song: “你介灯笼咋不圆呢？”

提出在VR里捏中国元素空间的主意，在长安城项目里和momo共同构思项目的取景焦点，元素建模，空间音效和“不正经打岔”

Song: “设计师和工程师的合作非常重要，我们在VR里即时合作的好处就是随时沟通检验想法，Austin实现脚本功能我们可以很直观看到然后一起调整，一些技术上实现不了的功能在比较早期也可以早发现避免。”

Momo Wang  王卯卯

动画导演，照明娱乐动画创意总监，兔斯基作者

LA Animation & Creative Director @illuminationent.
Momo Wang Scholarship @CalArts. 
Host @HiHollywoodArtHouse. 
Creator of Tuzki兔斯基 @clubtuzki.

在Horizon World长安城的项目里负责提案选题、主持会议和元素建模

Momo：“我和@SONG 见面那次成了horizon 官网群至今最热贴之一。”
Austin 孙博 

Lead data architect @TorqataDataandAnalytics
Founder @AsianAmericanLeadersassociation (non-profit)
BS+MS in Computer Science
BA+MBA in Business

在长安项目里负责动画脚本，帮助设计师和艺术家把想象中的效果实现出来。

Austin： “只要想的到，就能实现到。”

HuaPlus’s Production

Producer Brian Bai
Executive Producer Shuyi Wang
Research Alice Liu
Interview Shuyi Wang
Translation Alice Liu Chao Wang
Editing Shuyi Wang
VFX Xia Sheng

Marketing and Development
Rong Wang
Yanjun Xu
Xinyan Fu

"Chang'An, China (7th Century)" - Horizon Worlds  
Designed and Created by

Momo Wang
Chenxiang Song
Austin Sun

snippet.title: 星研所 | 專訪張超 | 棋士從容 INTERVIEW ON ZHANG CHAO
snippet.description:
張超認可自己的職業生涯有階段性。於選秀出身，在人氣最高的時候跑去製作音樂，接著轉型成為演員。他的職業生涯像流水一樣高低起伏，又途徑不同的領域——被打亂、被認知，接著重新排序，逐漸成為如今的模樣。
宣傳期的藝人或多或少會帶著已經回答成慣性的答案來應對大同小異的問題：飾演的角色，扮演的心路，還有劇本主創團隊等等……我們準備了很多問題想同他聊一聊《棋魂》這部劇，但見到他的那刻卻與想象中的不同。他說話的聲音很輕，動作優雅，像在沈澱著思考來進行對話，繼而讓人想起《棋魂》裏行走在南梁宮廷的第一棋士——褚贏。在「慣性的答案」之外，張超展現給我們他的另一面——抽離的藝術家。
